title: madhuca korthalsii var. lanceolata
intro: Madhuca korthalsii var. lanceolata, also known as the lanceolate form of the madhuca tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, specifically in Malaysia and Indonesia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Madhuca korthalsii var. lanceolata include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and skin infections. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
